2026-04-23T06:05:40.7034093Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-23T06:05:40.7034624Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-23T06:05:40.7035037Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-23T06:05:40.7035406Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-23T06:05:40.7035710Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-23T06:05:40.7035946Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-23T06:05:40.7036111Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-23T06:05:40.7036321Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-23T06:05:40.7036496Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-23T06:05:40.7036731Z [36;1m{[0m

2026-04-23T06:05:40.7037218Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-23T06:05:40.7037476Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

This introduced a new `tsp-location.yaml` pointing to `specification/frontdoor/resource-manager/Microsoft.Network/FrontDoor`, regenerated all code under `src/Generated/`, and bumped the package to `1.5.0-beta.1`. The key resource types now include `FrontDoorResource`, `FrontDoorWebApplicationFirewallPolicyResource`, `FrontDoorRulesEngineResource`, `FrontDoorNetworkExperimentProfileResource`, `FrontendEndpointResource`, and `FrontDoorExperimentResource`.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **No inline code examples in README** — the `## Examples` section only links to a generic samples page; there are no snippet placeholders and no `tests/Samples/` directory with `#region Snippet:` tagged methods\\n- **\\u0026#34;Key concepts\\u0026#34; section is empty/generic** — it links to the SDK introduction page but does not describe the FrontDoor-specific resource types and their relationships (FrontDoor → FrontendEndpoints, RulesEngines; WAF Policies; Network Experiment Profiles and Experiments)\\n- **CHANGELOG 1.5.0-beta.1 is empty** — the MPG migration is a meaningful change (TypeSpec-based regeneration, new API shape) and should be documented under `### Other Changes` or `### Features Added`\\n- **No `tests/Samples/` directory** — there are auto-generated samples under `tests/Generated/Samples/` but no hand-authored sample files with snippet markers usable by `Update-Snippets.ps1`\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### 1a. Update `sdk/frontdoor/Azure.ResourceManager.FrontDoor/README.md`\\n\\nEdit the file. Replace the generic `## Key concepts` section and update `## Examples` as shown below.\\n\\n**Replace** the existing `## Key concepts` block:\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\nKey concepts of the Microsoft Azure SDK for .NET can be found [here]((azure.github.io/redacted)\\n```\\n\\n**With:**\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\n`Azure.ResourceManager.FrontDoor` provides resource management support for Microsoft Azure Front Door services. The main resource types are:\\n\\n| Resource class | Description |\\n|---|---|\\n| `FrontDoorResource` | Represents a Front Door load-balancer/CDN instance with routing rules, backend pools, and frontend endpoints |\\n| `FrontendEndpointResource` | A frontend hostname (CNAME) attached to a Front Door |\\n| `FrontDoorRulesEngineResource` | A rules-engine configuration that allows advanced request-routing conditions on a Front Door |\\n| `FrontDoorWebApplicationFirewallPolicyResource` | A WAF policy that can be linked to a Front Door frontend endpoint |\\n| `FrontDoorNetworkExperimentProfileResource` | A Network Experiment profile used to measure latency between clients and Azure endpoints |\\n| `FrontDoorExperimentResource` | An individual experiment inside a Network Experiment profile |\\n\\nAll resource instances are obtained through an `ArmClient`:\\n\\n```\\nArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n```\\n```\\n\\n**Replace** the existing `## Examples` block:\\n```markdown\\n## Examples\\n\\nCode samples for using the management library for .NET can be found in the following locations\\n- [.NET Management Library Code Samples]((aka.ms/redacted)\\n```\\n\\n**With:**\\n```markdown\\n## Examples\\n\\n### Create or update a Front Door\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Get a WAF Policy\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### List all Front Doors in a subscription\\n\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n\\n#### 1b. Update `sdk/frontdoor/Azure.ResourceManager.FrontDoor/CHANGELOG.md`\\n\\nEdit the file. Fill in the empty `1.5.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` section:\\n\\n**Replace:**\\n```markdown\\n## 1.5.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)\\n\\n### Features Added\\n\\n### Breaking Changes\\n\\n### Bugs Fixed\\n\\n### Other Changes\\n```\\n\\n**With:**\\n```markdown\\n## 1.5.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)\\n\\n### Other Changes\\n\\n- Migrated SDK code generation from AutoRest to TypeSpec (Management Plane Generator). The public API surface is unchanged; internal serialization and client pipeline infrastructure now use the TypeSpec-based generator.\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nCreate a new file at `sdk/frontdoor/Azure.ResourceManager.FrontDoor/tests/Samples/Sample1_FrontDoor.cs` with the following content:\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\\n\\nusing System;\\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\\nusing Azure.Core;\\nusing Azure.Identity;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.FrontDoor;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.FrontDoor.Models;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.Resources;\\nusing NUnit.Framework;\\n\\nnamespace Azure.ResourceManager.FrontDoor.Tests.Samples\\n{\\n    public class Sample1_FrontDoor\\n    {\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task GetArmClient()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:FrontDoor_GetArmClient\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task CreateOrUpdate()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:FrontDoor_CreateOrUpdate\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            string subscriptionId = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n            string resourceGroupName = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n            string frontDoorName = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n\\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await client\\n                .GetSubscriptionResource(new ResourceIdentifier($\\u0026#34;/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}\\u0026#34;))\\n                .GetResourceGroupAsync(resourceGroupName);\\n\\n            FrontDoorCollection frontDoors = resourceGroup.GetFrontDoors();\\n\\n            FrontDoorData data = new FrontDoorData(new AzureLocation(\\u0026#34;global\\u0026#34;))\\n            {\\n                FriendlyName = frontDoorName,\\n            };\\n\\n            ArmOperation lro = await frontDoors.CreateOrUpdateAsync(\\n                WaitUntil.Completed,\\n                frontDoorName,\\n                data);\\n            FrontDoorResource frontDoor = lro.Value;\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Created Front Door: {frontDoor.Data.Name}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task GetWafPolicy()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:FrontDoor_GetWafPolicy\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            string subscriptionId = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n            string resourceGroupName = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n            string policyName = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n\\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await client\\n                .GetSubscriptionResource(new ResourceIdentifier($\\u0026#34;/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}\\u0026#34;))\\n                .GetResourceGroupAsync(resourceGroupName);\\n\\n            FrontDoorWebApplicationFirewallPolicyResource policy =\\n                await resourceGroup\\n                    .GetFrontDoorWebApplicationFirewallPolicies()\\n                    .GetAsync(policyName);\\n\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;WAF Policy mode: {policy.Data.PolicySettings?.Mode}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task ListAll()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:FrontDoor_ListAll\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            string subscriptionId = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n\\n            SubscriptionResource subscription = await client\\n                .GetSubscriptionResource(new ResourceIdentifier($\\u0026#34;/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}\\u0026#34;))\\n                .GetAsync();\\n\\n            await foreach (FrontDoorResource frontDoor in subscription.GetFrontDoorsAsync())\\n            {\\n                Console.WriteLine(frontDoor.Data.Name);\\n            }\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README currently has all required sections. The following sections need content improvements:\\n\\n| Section | Status | Action |\\n|---|---|---|\\n| Getting started – Install | ✅ Present | No change |\\n| Getting started – Prerequisites | ✅ Present | No change |\\n| Getting started – Authenticate | ✅ Present | No change |\\n| Key concepts | ⚠️ Generic only | Add FrontDoor-specific resource table (see Step 1) |\\n| Examples | ⚠️ No code | Add snippet placeholders (see Step 1) |\\n| Troubleshooting | ✅ Present | No change |\\n| Next steps | ✅ Present | No change |\\n| Contributing | ✅ Present | No change |\\n\\nReference: `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md`\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order from the repository root:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/frontdoor/Azure.ResourceManager.FrontDoor/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/frontdoor/Azure.ResourceManager.FrontDoor/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 frontdoor` — injects the `#region Snippet:` code blocks into the README placeholders; verify with `git diff sdk/frontdoor/Azure.ResourceManager.FrontDoor/README.md`\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 frontdoor` — regenerates public API listing files (only needed if any public types were added or changed)\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/frontdoor/Azure.ResourceManager.FrontDoor/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; - `centralus2.in.applicationinsights.azure.com`\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; To allow these domains, add them to the `network.allowed` list in your workflow frontmatter:\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; ```yaml\\n\\u0026gt; network:\\n\\u0026gt;   allowed:\\n\\u0026gt;     - defaults\\n\\u0026gt;     - \\u0026#34;centralus2.in.applicationinsights.azure.com\\u0026#34;\\n\\u0026gt; ```\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; See [Network Configuration](https://github.github.com/gh-aw/reference/network/) for more information.\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully."
2026-04-23T06:07:56.2388743Z       },
2026-04-23T06:07:56.2388826Z       {
